title: Kronecker Coefficients / Combinatorial Interpretation of Schur Function Plethysms
statement_text: There is no known combinatorial formula for the coefficients of Schur function plethysms (known as Kronecker coefficients) except in special cases.
context_text: The abstract identifies finding coefficients of Schur function plethysms as a major open question in symmetric function theory. Various combinatorial interpretations have been proposed (Nava–Rota via partitionals; Carré–Leclerc via crystal structures of domino tableaux), and searching for a satisfactory interpretation is described as an extremely active area of research, citing [7, 33].
paper_contribution: Introduces a graph-theoretic combinatorial interpretation for any plethysm of the form $X_{(G,w)}[f]$ (Theorem 2), expressed as a sum over pairs of acyclic orientations $\gamma$ of $G$ and maps $\kappa : V(G) \to \mathrm{Var}(f)$ satisfying monotonicity and properness conditions with respect to a total order on $\mathrm{Var}(f)$. Because chromatic symmetric functions of connected vertex-weighted graphs are algebraically independent generators of $\Lambda$, this extends to a combinatorial interpretation of any plethysm of symmetric functions, including Schur function plethysms.
notes: The open problem is not attributed to specific authors in the text and carries no formal conjecture label; cited as [7, 33]. Sections 3–6 of the paper are largely absent from the provided PDF extract, so any additional conjectures or open questions stated by the authors in Section 6 (further directions for research) cannot be recovered.
paper_title: Plethysms of Chromatic and Tutte Symmetric Functions
